So i have been working with this for a while now, and i find that when the MACD histogram goes red, it is a GREAT exit signal.  BUT, I added a Stoch RSI to the chart in a higher time frame (4x)instead of regularRSI. Now, that is my exit signal. When it drops sharply towards 60, sell. However, if the MACD histogram turns bright red and big bars first, sell on that signal. I have seen that the histogram goes red with small bars, but the 4x timeframe Stoch RSI stays above 60, so i hold longer. 
I have been checking these against the S&P 500 on a daily chart, so results may differ on different stocks, but they have been working extremely well. I backtest using the REPLAY function on Trading View to make my backtesting more real, and they have performed well. Most trades are profitable but it is not performing as well over a year as buy and hold :(
But, if you want a strategy that will work on any stock, this should do it. Also, they should work on all time frames, just remember to make the extra Stoch RSI 4 times timeframe. Play with it and reply ;)
